title: leucanthemopsis alpina var. minima
intro: Leucanthemopsis alpina var. minima, also known as Alpine Cushion Daisy or Dwarf Alpine Daisy, is a small, perennial plant that belongs to the daisy family. It is native to the mountains of Europe, where it grows at high altitudes. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which are believed to help soothe skin irritations and protect against oxidative stress. Leucanthemopsis alpina var. minima can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, which are often used to support skin health, reduce inflammation, and promote overall well-being.
